justification: Both domain rules (state transition SHIPPED→PENDING and refund precondition on DELIVERED) are present with specific, testable assertions. All three architecture decisions (PATCH endpoint, OrderStateMachine, RefundService) are present with detailed implementation notes — exceeding the two required. Acceptance criteria preserve the error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID verbatim in the first criterion. Terminology alignment is present and addresses the Bird/MJ language differences, mapping domain concepts to technical implementations. Task context is one specific, actionable paragraph. No invented information — all details trace back to the described source summaries. The only minor note is that terminology_alignment focuses on domain-to-technical mappings rather than explicitly calling out that Bird says 'SHIPPED' and MJ says 'PATCH /orders/{id}/state' refer to the same concept, but the 'order state' entry and 'revert-to-pending' entry effectively bridge this gap.

observations.text: Both domain invariants present with precise, testable assertions that specify exact behavior (throw/return error, reject before record creation)
Three architecture decisions provided instead of the minimum two, with RefundService explicitly covered including fail-closed behavior
ORDER_STATE_INVALID error code preserved verbatim in acceptance criteria
Given/When/Then format maintained with specific HTTP status codes and behavioral expectations
Terminology alignment bridges domain language to technical implementation with three well-defined mappings
task_context is a single focused paragraph explaining what, why, and the key constraints
open_questions section shows intellectual honesty — flags gaps without inventing answers
No fabricated information detected; all content is traceable to expected source material
terminology_alignment could more explicitly state that Bird's 'order status' and MJ's 'state' field are the same concept — the mapping is implicit rather than called out as a reconciliation

    cannot move backward from SHIPPED to PENDING, and refunds are gated on DELIVERED
